paragraph: The single-family market in Little Ferry is balanced and steady. With 16 sales over six months at a median of $598,000, homes are selling right at asking price, and half are fetching offers above list. The median days on market sits at 42, which means priced homes are moving but without the urgency of a true seller's market. Multi-family properties tell a different story. Only 5 sales over the period, but they're commanding attention. The median sits at $730,000, sale-to-list ratio climbs to 1.02, and median days on market drops to 22. Sixty percent sold above asking. This tier is competitive, though the sample is small enough that any single sale moves the needle. Condos and townhouses are the volume play at 14 sales and a median of $292,250. Like single-family, they're selling at parity with list price and moving in 19 days on the median. Half sold above asking. This is the entry point in Little Ferry and it's working. If you're weighing single-family against multi-family against condo, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family, the income or flexibility of a multi-family building, or the lower price point of a condo.
